decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper conducts a scoping review specifically assessing Mpox surveillance systems, structures, reporting strategies, and implementation challenges.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete infectious-disease application of surveillance evidence synthesis to Mpox in endemic African regions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review identifies and narratively synthesizes HAI surveillance tools, organization, laboratory methods, and standardization gaps.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete review of healthcare-associated-infection surveillance in the WHO African Region.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article uses a narrative synthesis to identify surveillance and response deficiencies and derive evidence-based outbreak recommendations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly recommends locally aligned surveillance, stronger laboratories, revised case definitions, and improved outbreak-data systems.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The literature review concerns needlestick-injury reporting behavior, not synthesis or guidance about infectious-disease surveillance methods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Underreporting is an outcome discussed, but the record is not a lineage source for surveillance evidence synthesis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review synthesizes zoonotic-tuberculosis epidemiology and transmission; surveillance integration is only one identified policy gap.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It does not explicitly develop, evaluate, or apply a surveillance evidence-synthesis method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The scoping review synthesizes gonorrhea prevalence, resistance, and laboratory testing rather than evidence about surveillance systems or surveillance-review methods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Its recommendation to strengthen sentinel and AMR surveillance does not make the review a lineage source for this concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic search directly diagnoses fragmentation, heterogeneity, geographic ascertainment differences, and underreporting in global gonococcal AMR surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It evaluates how surveillance-system strength and data sharing distort observed resistance prevalence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review evaluates human-to-human leptospirosis transmission; surveillance appears only as a concluding recommendation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is not a source about surveillance evidence-synthesis methods or their performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review jointly analyzes official surveillance reports and published research data to characterize echinococcosis and quantify underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete synthesis of Serbian human and animal surveillance evidence for an infectious disease.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article combines routine and enhanced data to estimate zoonotic-disease burden, but does not explicitly apply or evaluate a surveillance evidence-review method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Underreporting and underdiagnosis are limitations of the burden data, not a developed surveillance-synthesis contribution.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review explicitly synthesizes empirical evidence on dengue surveillance tools for outbreak detection, prediction, and trend monitoring.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It derives detailed recommendations for passive, sentinel, syndromic, laboratory, and event-based surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The narrative review integrates national case reports and three named sentinel or behavioral surveillance programs to assess gonorrhea trends among MSM.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete synthesis of multiple US gonorrhea surveillance data sources and their complementary biases.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The title explicitly presents best-practice guidance for leveraging sentinel surveillance systems to track COVID-19.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The title identifies the record as surveillance-system guidance derived from LMIC experience; no abstract supplies further detail.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review critically analyzes regional dengue surveillance strategies, identifying underreporting, absent integration, and operational delays.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It directly evaluates weaknesses of vector and disease surveillance and proposes corrective system designs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The scoping review synthesizes the development, testing, functions, and effects of digital tools for post-discharge SSI surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete evidence synthesis of digital surgical-site-infection surveillance implementations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The comprehensive review explicitly examines tick-borne-disease surveillance methods, systems, challenges, innovations, and future directions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It provides method-oriented surveillance guidance spanning passive, active, laboratory, sentinel, syndromic, and One Health approaches.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review evaluates the routine utility and effectiveness of syndromic surveillance for early detection and monitoring of gastrointestinal infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It synthesizes validation and performance evidence and identifies unresolved system-specific limitations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The scoping review examines COVID-19 effects on malaria-control programs broadly; surveillance is only one of five thematic areas.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It does not explicitly study a surveillance evidence-synthesis method or surveillance-system performance as its exact subject.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review and meta-analysis benchmarks AI-based HAI surveillance against manual and automated alternatives using pooled performance and implementation measures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It directly evaluates accuracy, clinical deployment, impact evidence, interpretability, and reproducibility of AI surveillance systems.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article advocates enhanced occupational zoonosis surveillance but does not explicitly conduct a surveillance-focused evidence synthesis or evaluate such a review method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The scoping review synthesizes evidence on integrating COVID-19 into sentinel systems and evaluates detection, representativeness, adaptability, and sustainability.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It directly assesses the effects and operational performance of a surveillance-system integration strategy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The narrative synthesis explicitly evaluates WNV surveillance approaches and reporting differences while identifying integration and preparedness gaps.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It diagnoses how surveillance intensity, diagnostic capacity, and reporting frameworks affect notified case counts.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The non-systematic literature appraisal synthesizes evidence on absent reporting and epidemiological-surveillance systems for laboratory-acquired infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review systematically identifies reported African bacterial HAI outbreaks and uses the sparse evidence to characterize under-detection and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete infectious-disease evidence synthesis centered on outbreak-reporting gaps.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review addresses HIV–syphilis testing and management; underreporting is mentioned only as a possible explanation for reduced diagnoses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It does not synthesize or evaluate surveillance gaps as an exact methodological subject.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The record synthesizes 12 country narratives and identifies recurring child-TB under-detection, underreporting, and service-reporting gaps.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review synthesizes regional meningococcal surveillance practices and explains how case definitions, confirmation methods, and laboratory barriers produce misreporting or underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The narrative review and expert panel explicitly synthesize gaps in adult pertussis incidence, recognition, reporting, complications, and vaccine evidence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: An international expert panel synthesizes challenges in pertussis detection and monitoring, including under-ascertainment caused by nonstandard testing and case definitions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record presents expert guidance on practical detection, monitoring, and vaccination challenges.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review analyzes Chandipura outbreak epidemiology; potential underreporting is only a stated limitation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It does not specifically synthesize surveillance gaps or evaluate surveillance methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review links regional TBE incidence variation to underreporting and diagnostic access and explicitly evaluates needed improvements to Poland's surveillance system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The clinician guide focuses on pertussis diagnosis, treatment, and vaccination; underreporting is an incidental epidemiological challenge.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review identifies geographic discrepancies and underreporting in Mediterranean finfish disease evidence and links them to diagnostic capacity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The global prevalence review merely lists limited surveillance or underreporting among several possible explanations for sparse regional evidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It does not explicitly synthesize or evaluate surveillance gaps as its exact subject.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review argues for national foodborne-disease burden estimation; surveillance and underreporting are supporting governance considerations rather than the exact synthesis subject.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The narrative review synthesizes surveillance reports and outbreak investigations to examine detection, response, underreporting, and preparedness gaps.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The structured review critically synthesizes TB surveillance and reporting limitations, pandemic disruptions, undernotification, and digital-platform implementation challenges.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review merely notes that broader geographic evidence is needed to ensure generalizability; it does not use a transportability or generalizability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Generalizability is a limitation and research need, not a methodological contribution.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors propose and illustrate a model-based standardization method for generalizing trial results to a specified target population and validate it by simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological-development paper for quantitative generalizability from trials to target populations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The cohort study only lists characteristics that may limit generalizability; no generalizability or transportability method is used.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an applied HIV-transmission study, not a lineage lead for this concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review explicitly evaluates included implementation studies by geographical and institutional setting and generalizability to country-level scale-up.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete TB-control application of structured generalizability assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article identifies generalizability as a cluster-trial challenge and proposes respondent-driven sampling for recruitment, not a transportability/generalizability method itself.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Its methodological contribution concerns respondent-driven sampling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The mixed-methods guideline-support study only acknowledges limited generalizability and reliability of end-user evidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No transportability or generalizability analysis is performed.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The commentary explicitly examines generalizability and transportability from randomized trials to new settings and uses causal DAGs to articulate sufficient conditions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological guidance on assumptions for transporting PrEP trial effects to real-world target populations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The meta-analysis states that geographic imbalance limits pooled-estimate generalizability but does not apply a generalizability or transportability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Generalizability is reported only as a limitation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The surveillance-cohort study says broader generalizability is unknown; it does not conduct a transportability or generalizability analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an applied mortality-trends study with a generalizability caveat.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study standardizes cohort effect estimates to a national surveillance-defined target population using methods for generalizing between populations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV application of quantitative generalizability methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The historical review notes difficulties in generalizability but does not develop or apply an exact transportability/generalizability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Its primary contribution is a broad history of TB epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study explicitly tests the external generalizability of Utah serotype findings using additional studies from the United States and Europe.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete cross-population generalizability assessment of pneumococcal serotype dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper states assumptions for generalizing test-negative controls to population coverage, examines bias by simulation, and validates against external data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete vaccine-coverage application of generalizability assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The modeling study only identifies limited generalizability caused by missing data and heterogeneity; it does not use a generalizability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Generalizability is an acknowledged limitation of the bedaquiline model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The observational comparison explicitly accounts for generalizability by standardizing measured variables alongside confounding and selection bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-treatment application of standardization for generalizability.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The randomized trial only states that generalizability beyond its single clinic is uncertain.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No transportability or generalizability method is applied.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The cohort restricts follow-up to improve contemporary relevance but does not perform a formal generalizability or transportability analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Generalizability motivates a design choice rather than constituting the method studied.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The single-site cohort lists lack of generalizability as a limitation without applying a transportability or generalizability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an applied ART-retention study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The cluster trial only notes limited generalizability beyond similar female-sex-worker settings.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No generalizability or transportability method is used.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The modeling study says outbreak heterogeneity limits estimate generalizability but does not apply a transportability/generalizability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Generalizability is only a limitation of the cholera simulations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study evaluates qSOFA predictive validity across nine diverse cohorts and multiple settings to assess cross-setting performance and potential generalizability.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The continuation trial only states uncertain generalizability outside hyperendemic trachoma regions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper explicitly evaluates the generalizability of its nonlinear time-series threshold approach using data from five European populations and multiple epidemiological phases.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The pragmatic trial compares community and clinic delivery but only cites single-clinic generalizability and carryover as limitations; no formal generalizability method is stated.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper applies causal transportability theory, causal graphs, and selection diagrams to diagnose when HIV implementation results generalize to a target setting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological overview and guidance for using the transportability framework in implementation science.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The randomized trial merely requests replication and assessment of generalizability after reporting its intervention effect.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No generalizability or transportability method is applied.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The pooled trial analysis only notes limitations in generalizability to routine vaccine use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It does not apply a transportability or generalizability method.

decisions.lineage_evidence: It provides explicit methodological guidance for interpreting and improving the generalizability of prevalence studies.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The framework concerns standardized reporting of agent-based models; generalizability is an intended benefit rather than a method it applies or develops.
